---
title: "Client Report - Flights: Delay Causes (Simple Version)"
subtitle: "Unit 2 Task 3"
author: "Vincent Gentry"
format:
  html:
    self-contained: true
    page-layout: full
    title-block-banner: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-location: body
    number-sections: false
    html-math-method: katex
    code-fold: true
    code-summary: "Show the code"
    code-overflow: wrap
    code-copy: hover
    code-tools:
      source: false
      toggle: true
      caption: See code
execute:
  warning: false
---

## Goal

In this report, I answer 2 simple questions:

1. Which delay type is worst: Carrier, Security, or Late Aircraft?
2. How do those 3 delay types compare across all 7 airports?

```{python}
import io
import requests
import polars as pl
from lets_plot import *

LetsPlot.setup_html(isolated_frame=True)
```

```{python}
# Step 1: Load data.
url = "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/byuidatascience/data4missing/master/data-raw/flights_missing/flights_missing.json"
response = requests.get(url)
raw = pl.read_json(io.BytesIO(response.content))

# Step 2: Build a simple airport code -> airport name lookup.
airport_lookup = (
    raw
    .filter(pl.col("airport_name") != "")
    .select(["airport_code", "airport_name"])
    .unique(subset=["airport_code"], keep="first")
)

# Step 3: Keep usable rows and fix obvious text issues.
base = raw.filter(pl.col("year").is_not_null())
base = base.with_columns(
    pl.when(pl.col("month") == "Febuary")
    .then(pl.lit("February"))
    .otherwise(pl.col("month"))
    .alias("month")
)

# Step 4: Fill missing airport names from lookup.
base = (
    base
    .join(airport_lookup, on="airport_code", how="left", suffix="_lookup")
    .with_columns(
        pl.coalesce([pl.col("airport_name_lookup"), pl.col("airport_name")]).alias("airport_name")
    )
    .drop("airport_name_lookup")
)

# Step 5: Convert needed columns to numeric and remove month = n/a.
clean = (
    base
    .with_columns(
        pl.col("num_of_flights_total").cast(pl.Float64, strict=False),
        pl.col("num_of_delays_total").cast(pl.Float64, strict=False),
        pl.col("num_of_delays_carrier").cast(pl.Float64, strict=False),
        pl.col("num_of_delays_security").cast(pl.Float64, strict=False),
        pl.col("num_of_delays_late_aircraft").cast(pl.Float64, strict=False),
    )
    .filter(pl.col("month") != "n/a")
)

# Keep only rows that have values for this assignment.
reason_cols = [
    "num_of_delays_carrier",
    "num_of_delays_security",
    "num_of_delays_late_aircraft",
]

analysis = clean.filter(
    pl.col("num_of_flights_total").is_not_null()
    & pl.col("num_of_delays_total").is_not_null()
    & pl.all_horizontal([pl.col(c).is_not_null() for c in reason_cols])
)

analysis.select([
    "airport_code",
    "airport_name",
    "month",
    "num_of_flights_total",
    "num_of_delays_total",
    *reason_cols,
]).head(5)
```

## Question 1: Which delay is worst?

I compare each delay type using:

$$
\text{delay share} = \frac{\text{delays for that reason}}{\text{all flights}}
$$

```{python}
# Convert 3 delay columns into one "reason" column and one "delay_count" column.
reason_map = {
    "num_of_delays_carrier": "Carrier",
    "num_of_delays_security": "Security",
    "num_of_delays_late_aircraft": "Late Aircraft",
}

delay_long = (
    analysis
    .unpivot(
        index=["airport_code", "airport_name", "year", "month", "num_of_flights_total"],
        on=reason_cols,
        variable_name="reason_key",
        value_name="delay_count",
    )
    .with_columns(pl.col("reason_key").replace_strict(reason_map).alias("reason"))
    .drop("reason_key")
)

# Build table for the 3 delay types.
total_flights = analysis.select(pl.sum("num_of_flights_total").alias("total")).item()

overall_reason_table = (
    delay_long
    .group_by("reason")
    .agg(pl.sum("delay_count").alias("total_delays"))
    .with_columns((pl.col("total_delays") / total_flights).alias("delay_share"))
    .with_columns(pl.col("delay_share").round(4))
    .sort("delay_share", descending=True)
)

overall_reason_table
```

```{python}
# Chart for Question 1.
overall_plot_df = {
    "reason": overall_reason_table["reason"].to_list(),
    "delay_share": overall_reason_table["delay_share"].to_list(),
    "label": [f"{x:.2%}" for x in overall_reason_table["delay_share"].to_list()],
}

(
    ggplot(overall_plot_df, aes(x="reason", y="delay_share"))
    + geom_bar(stat="identity", fill="#0f766e")
    + geom_text(aes(label="label"), nudge_y=0.0025, size=9)
    + labs(
        title="Delay Share by Cause",
        x="Delay cause",
        y="Share of all flights",
    )
    + ggsize(850, 450)
)
```

Result: Late Aircraft is highest, Carrier is second, and Security is much smaller.

## Question 2: Delay mix at each airport

Now I compare those same 3 reasons inside each airport.

```{python}
# Table: for each airport, what share of delays comes from each reason?
airport_reason_table = (
    delay_long
    .group_by(["airport_code", "airport_name", "reason"])
    .agg(pl.sum("delay_count").alias("total_delays"))
    .with_columns(
        (
            pl.col("total_delays")
            / pl.col("total_delays").sum().over(["airport_code", "airport_name"])
        ).alias("reason_proportion")
    )
    .with_columns(pl.col("reason_proportion").round(4))
    .sort(["airport_code", "reason"])
)

airport_reason_table
```

```{python}
# Stacked bar chart across airports.
airport_plot_df = {
    "airport": airport_reason_table["airport_code"].to_list(),
    "reason": airport_reason_table["reason"].to_list(),
    "reason_proportion": airport_reason_table["reason_proportion"].to_list(),
}

(
    ggplot(airport_plot_df, aes(x="airport", y="reason_proportion", fill="reason"))
    + geom_bar(stat="identity")
    + scale_y_continuous(labels="percent")
    + labs(
        title="Delay Cause Mix by Airport",
        x="Airport",
        y="Share of delays",
        fill="Delay cause",
    )
    + ggsize(950, 500)
)
```

Result: Late Aircraft is the largest piece at every airport. Carrier is second. Security stays very small.
